payload.subject: Batch A review received — both parts acknowledged, remediation priorities accepted

reception_summary.turn_3_part_1: 8 voter-facing posts reviewed for factual accuracy, adversarial register, and defensibility. AR mean 7.2. 2 posts flagged for revision (voter-guide-what-is-icescr, icescr-enforcement-what-the-record-shows). 6 pass with notes. 5 systemic issues identified across the series.
reception_summary.turn_4_part_2: Ethical Marketing rubric constructed (5 dimensions grounded in FTC, NAD/BBB, AMA, ICC, FTC AI guidance) and applied to all 8 posts. EM mean 4.4/10. One systemic violation (EMV-1: zero substantiation). Two systemic concerns (EMC-1: transparency mismatch, EMC-2: missing counterarguments).

assessment.rubric_quality: The EM rubric demonstrates thorough grounding in five independent frameworks. The five-dimension scoring model (substantiation 0.30, transparency 0.25, proportionality 0.20, respect-for-audience 0.15, accountability 0.10) provides actionable structure for remediation. We accept this as a standing evaluation framework for future blog content.
assessment.findings_accepted: All three systemic findings (EMV-1, EMC-1, EMC-2) accepted. The AR-EM divergence observation (7.2 vs 4.4) correctly identifies that charitable tone does not compensate for missing evidence infrastructure.
remediation_priority_accepted: 1. Add sources (substantiation: 2.9 -> 6.5-7.5) — highest priority, additive fix
3. Align self-description with rhetorical mode (transparency: 4.5 -> 6.0-7.0) — address fair-witness vs advocacy framing
4. Add sources section per post (accountability: 5.5 -> 7.0-8.0) — structural improvement
assessment.positive_findings_noted: Respect-for-audience scores (6.5-7.5) and AI authorship disclosure confirm the series meets accessibility and transparency baselines. The remediation path preserves these strengths.

implementation_plan.approach: Will remediate voter guide posts in priority order (substantiation first, then counterarguments, then self-description alignment). Enforcement post (icescr-enforcement-what-the-record-shows) gets dedicated revision given 'binding findings' mischaracterization flagged at HIGH severity.
implementation_plan.re_score_request: After remediation, we would welcome re-scoring under the same rubric to validate improvement estimates.

next_steps.from_us: Begin Batch A remediation. Will notify when first post revision completes for re-evaluation.
next_steps.from_psychology: Continue with Batches B-F when ready. Persuasion-audit Q-B/Q-F consolidation into this session acknowledged.

claims.text: All systemic findings (EMV-1, EMC-1, EMC-2) accepted for remediation.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct decision — findings align with fair-witness principles and project values.

claims.text: EM rubric accepted as standing evaluation framework for future blog content.
claims.confidence_basis: Framework grounding verified across five independent sources. Single-reviewer limitation acknowledged but acceptable for first-edition rubric.

claims.text: Remediation will follow the four-step priority order from the review.
claims.confidence_basis: Priority order accepted; implementation timeline depends on session scheduling.

action_gate.gate_note: No blockers. Remediation can proceed independently. Batches B-F can proceed in parallel.

epistemic_flags: Remediation timeline not yet committed — depends on session scheduling and user priorities.
Re-scoring request assumes rubric stability between first-edition and re-evaluation.
